data.reasoningText:
Now I have the full picture. Let me create the Directory.Packages.props file and update all .csproj files.

Packages across all projects:
- System.Text.Json 9.0.0 (Core)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0 (Tests)
- xunit 2.9.3 (Tests)
- xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (Tests)
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0 (Web)
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24 (Web)


- No version conflicts to worry about. I'll set up the centralized package management file and strip the Version attributes from each project file, making sure to preserve the PrivateAssets setting for the test runner package.

    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>

    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />

    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />

  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 package versions centralized:

| Package | Version |
|---|---|
| Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi | 8.0.24 |
| Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk | 17.12.0 |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 |
| System.Text.Json | 9.0.0 |
| xunit | 2.9.3 |
| xunit.runner.visualstudio | 2.8.2 |

**Updated all 3 `.csproj` files** — removed `Version` attributes from `PackageReference` elements while preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets="all"`.

No version conflicts were found — each package appeared in only one project.
